title: ‘Stay away’: Elders, imams, functionaries urged Muslim youth to skip CJP protest
content: Ahead of a recent demonstration, a significant section of the Muslim community received widespread warnings urging them to stay away. Social media and mosque announcements advised against participation, citing potential long-term consequences and the risk of being scapegoated if unrest occurred. This caution reflects a growing sentiment following past protests and investigations.

title: Indian man shot dead in US after late-night pizza delivery in Philadelphia
content: Tragedy struck North Philadelphia as Indian delivery driver Anshul Kuncha was fatally shot after a pizza delivery to a vacant unit.  Investigators are pursuing a lead from the phone number used for the order. Surveillance footage shows two individuals following Kuncha before the incident. No arrests have been made.

title: Watch: Eggs hurled at TMC MLA Madan Mitra's vehicle amid protest in Kamarhati
content: Senior TMC MLA Madan Mitra faced protests in Ariadaha, with residents allegedly hurling eggs at his vehicle amid demands for repayment of "cut money." This incident follows a pattern of public anger targeting TMC leaders post-election defeat, fueled by corruption allegations. Similar demonstrations have occurred across the state, reflecting widespread discontent.

title: 'Trailer of what is coming next': CJP says 'cockroaches showed their strength' in first protest
content: Hundreds gathered at Jantar Mantar demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over examination and recruitment irregularities. The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) called the protest a 'trailer' for a larger movement, vowing fresh demonstrations if Pradhan isn't sacked within seven days. Participants wore cockroach masks, highlighting concerns over NEET, CUET, and SSC processes.

title: Google CEO to entry-level graduates: I don't agree with the troubled AI scenario
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai offers a positive outlook for new graduates amidst AI advancements. He sees AI as a tool that will enhance individual capabilities, making skills more accessible. Pichai also highlights AI's potential to reduce workplace burnout, particularly in demanding professions. While acknowledging disruption, he encourages optimism about AI's positive impact on future careers.

title: ViKing of Norway: Pragg wins crown that eluded even Vishy
content: R Praggnanandhaa has achieved a historic victory at Norway Chess, a feat even Vishy Anand couldn't accomplish. The young Indian Grandmaster secured the title by defeating Vincent Keymer in the final round, overcoming fatigue from prior tournaments. His remarkable comeback, including four consecutive classical wins, has placed him among chess elite, proving his exceptional talent.

title: '5 buildings, 13 plots, mountain of cash': Rs 6k-salaried govt engineer's empire under scanner
content: Odisha Vigilance has unearthed assets worth crores, including over Rs 2.4 crore in cash, from an assistant executive engineer, Baikuntha Nath Behera. Raids across multiple locations revealed five high-rise buildings, 13 plots, gold ornaments, and significant bank deposits. The investigation into disproportionate assets is ongoing.

title: Monaco GP: Antonelli stuns Ferrari duo to snatch pole, Verstappen joins front row
content: Nineteen-year-old Kimi Antonelli secured a stunning pole position at the Monaco Grand Prix, defying expectations and Mercedes' first pole of the decade.  Despite Ferrari's strong practice pace, Antonelli's decisive final lap edged out Max Verstappen, setting the stage for a thrilling strategic battle.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
content: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

title: Hours after replacing Surya as India T20I captain, Shreyas leads side to win over SKY
content: Shreyas Iyer, newly appointed India's T20I captain, marked his first day by leading SoBo Mumbai Falcons to victory over Suryakumar Yadav's Triumph Knights MNE in the T20 Mumbai League. Iyer's commanding 61 off 36 balls steered his team to a five-wicket win, overshadowing Suryakumar's 48. Despite the captaincy change, Suryakumar expressed pride and support for Iyer's appointment.

title: LPG gets Rs 29 costlier: Check latest cylinder rates in Delhi, Mumbai and other cities
content: Domestic cooking gas prices have increased by Rs 29 per cylinder, reaching Rs 942 in Delhi, marking the second revision in three months due to soaring global energy costs. This rise follows a previous Rs 60 hike and is part of a broader trend of fuel price increases, with petrol and diesel also seeing significant jumps.

title: Iran peace deal at risk? US considers redirecting Iranian assets to Gulf states
content: The US is exploring redirecting frozen Iranian assets to Gulf states for rebuilding after missile and drone strikes. This move comes as indirect negotiations between the US and Iran remain deadlocked, with Tehran demanding the release of $24 billion in frozen funds. Military tensions continue to escalate across the region despite ongoing diplomatic efforts.

title: As Jamie Dimon calls Elon Musk 'Edison of our time', Musk says: There will not be much AC left
content: As SpaceX eyes a historic IPO, JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon likened Elon Musk to Thomas Edison. This sparked online debate, with many championing Nikola Tesla. Musk himself weighed in, acknowledging Edison's brilliance while asserting DC's future dominance over AC, citing solar, batteries, and EVs as key drivers.

title: Revenge killing, contract killers? Cops widen probe in DU professor murder
content: Police are investigating the murder of Delhi University professor Debosmita Paul, with her estranged husband claiming an alibi in Pune. Investigators are probing revenge and contract killing angles, reviewing CCTV footage showing two unidentified men entering the residential complex. Four individuals, including a masked duo, remain unidentified as the probe intensifies.

title: Beyond the bull run: Why affluent Indians are investing in premium homes
content: Wealthy Indian investors are shifting focus from equities to premium residential real estate, seeking stability and long-term value amid market volatility. Experts highlight that high-quality properties in prime locations are now viewed as a crucial asset for wealth preservation and growth, comparable to traditional investments.

title: Connor McDavid car collection: Supercars, luxury garage & lifestyle
content: Connor McDavid’s luxury garage is still the hot ticket for hockey fans in 2026, and the Edmonton Oilers captain is said to own multiple luxury cars, including Porsche, Lamborghini, Audi, Mercedes-Benz and Land Rover in the lineup. McDavid’s car collection is a glimpse into the lifestyle of one of the NHL’s biggest stars, along with his ever-growing net worth and endorsement portfolio.

title: Mirra Andreeva net worth: Earnings, endorsements & lifestyle after French Open win
content: Mirra Andreeva is one of the brightest young stars in women’s tennis. The 19-year-old Russian has enjoyed a breakthrough rise on the WTA Tour. She has reached the French Open 2026 final. She has delivered impressive performances at Grand Slams and WTA 1000 events. These wins have made her one of the most talked-about players in and off tennis court.

title: Why Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are linked to a July MSG wedding
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly set to tie the knot at New York City’s Madison Square Garden on July 3, 2026. The choice is driven by unparalleled privacy. With plans for underground access and street closures to keep paparazzi at bay. The iconic venue also holds personal significance for Swift. She has performed multiple times there. As for Kelce’s connection, he was recently spotted for a Knicks vs. Cavaliers game at MSG with Swift.

title: Israeli soldiers open fire at car in West Bank; 7-month-old infant dies
content: In a heartbreaking incident in Hebron, a Palestinian infant lost his life after Israeli soldiers opened fire on his family's vehicle. The father recounted how they had paused and raised their hands in surrender moments before the shots erupted. According to the IDF, the troops perceived the car as a potential threat due to its speed.

title: Kohli out, Jaiswal in: Former India cricketer calls it an 'embarrassment of riches'
content: Former cricketer Atul Wassan views Virat Kohli's absence from the Afghanistan ODI series as a strategic advantage. This break aids workload management for the 2027 World Cup and provides a platform for emerging talent like Yashasvi Jaiswal. Wassan believes preserving key players like Kohli and Bumrah is crucial for major tournaments, highlighting India's batting depth.

title: 731 people died in Manipur camps since ethnic conflict; 43,000 still displaced
content: Over 700 internally displaced persons have died in Manipur since May 2023, according to state home department data revealed through an RTI request. The information also indicates that over 43,000 people remain in relief camps and pre-fabricated housing, with significant health challenges reported among the displaced population.

title: How a murder in England by a Brit-Sikh triggered a MAGA meltdown
content: A British student's murder has ignited a transatlantic political firestorm. US Vice President JD Vance and Elon Musk have seized on the case, criticizing UK immigration and multicultural policies. This has sparked outrage in Britain, with accusations of American interference and a widening ideological rift between London and Washington.

title: Upasana Kamineni's 77,000 crore business empire
content: Upasana Kamineni, celebrated for her entrepreneurial spirit as the wife of superstar Ram Charan, is reshaping the healthcare landscape with her leadership at a thriving business empire that boasts a valuation of Rs 77,000 crore. Not only does she oversee her family's ventures, but she also plays a pivotal role in global life sciences and healthcare boards.

title: ‘Among those most educated ... ’: Elon Musk flags India’s falling fertility rate
content: India's fertility rate has slipped beneath the 2.1 children per woman threshold, with a majority of states now reflecting this trend. Elon Musk pointed out that educated women have long surpassed this level, indicating a significant demographic transition. This evolving landscape hints at a slower population increase and the looming concerns of an aging society.

title: Praggnanandhaa Exclusive: 'Winning ahead of Carlsen is something I've always wanted'
content: The 20-year-old from Chennai began the second half of the event from the bottom of the open-category standings, only to produce a remarkable comeback that included two classical victories over Carlsen, a win over the reigning world champion Dommaraju Gukesh and a final-round classical win over Germany No. 1 Vincent Keymer.

title: Is Israel spying on US? Iran talks come under spotlight as espionage concerns grow
content: US intelligence agencies are reportedly concerned about Israeli espionage targeting American negotiators involved in Iran peace talks. Recent assessments suggest intensified Israeli surveillance on senior US officials, raising counterintelligence threats to a critical level. This comes amid diverging US-Israeli objectives regarding Iran, potentially impacting future military cooperation.

title: 'I cut power': What chef told cops in Delhi hotel fire probe
content: A chef, Keshav Negi, has been arrested in connection with the Hauz Rani B&B fire that killed 21 people. Police suspect Negi's actions, including switching off the main power supply, may have contributed to the higher death toll by disabling the electronic door locks and trapping occupants.

title: Why India moved on from Suryakumar Yadav - The numbers behind the end of the SKY era
content: Three months after leading India to the T20 World Cup title, Suryakumar Yadav has lost both the captaincy and his place in India's T20I setup. The selectors insist the decision was driven by form and the need to look ahead to the next World Cup cycle. A deep dive into the numbers reveals why. From one of the greatest peaks in T20 history to declining returns, waning match-winning influence and rising competition from younger rivals, the data explains why India chose the future over the past.

title: 'Concentration of curry': Singapore blocks 14 anti-Indian posts likely from China
content: Singapore has ordered YouTube, Facebook, and X to block 14 posts targeting its Indian community and multiculturalism. The Ministry of Home Affairs stated the content, originating from overseas and likely China, aimed to sow discord by attacking the nation's multiracial society. These actions underscore Singapore's commitment to social cohesion and opposition to xenophobia.

title: Ed Sheeran's real journey: From small town dreamer to global star
content: Emerging from a life of hardship in London to become a global phenomenon, Ed Sheeran embodies the spirit of determination. Dismissed by record companies due to his unconventional style and sound, he pushed forward, driven by resilience. His heartfelt lyrics, forged in the tranquility of Suffolk, struck a chord worldwide, demonstrating that authenticity can overshadow commercial expectations.

title: Ohio mass shooting: Multiple injured in Toledo Old West End festival, manhunt under way
content: Twelve people were shot near Toledo's Old West End Festival on Saturday afternoon, prompting a large police response. All victims are expected to survive. Authorities are investigating the incident, which occurred during the popular community event, and are seeking information to identify the suspects.

title: Cardio or strength training: Harvard study finally picks one for longevity
content: A landmark Harvard study reveals strength training, not just cardio, is crucial for longevity. Aiming for 90-120 minutes weekly significantly slashes death risks from all causes, heart disease, and brain conditions. Combining strength with cardio offers the greatest health boost, proving muscle is vital for healthy aging and independence, not just aesthetics.

title: Police officers in UP more loyal to ruling dispensation than Constitution, says HC
content: The Allahabad High Court observed that Uttar Pradesh police officers exhibit loyalty to the ruling party over the Constitution, hindering constitutional governance. The court noted that transfers, postings, and promotions are often based on political patronage rather than merit, leading officers to prioritize political superiors' satisfaction over upholding the rule of law and due process.

title: Did Janhvi Kapoor oppose to her camera angles in Peddi? Co-actor Ram Charan clarifies
content: Screenshots allegedly showing Janhvi Kapoor expressing discomfort with certain camera angles during 'Peddi' filming have surfaced. The purported chats suggest she raised concerns about intimate shots, with co-star Ram Charan reportedly intervening. These leaked messages add a new dimension to the film's controversy, though their authenticity remains unconfirmed.

title: 'Very happy for Shreyas Iyer': Surya reacts after losing India captaincy
content: Suryakumar Yadav has graciously congratulated Shreyas Iyer on his appointment as India's new T20I captain for the upcoming Ireland and England series. Despite being replaced as captain and dropped from the squad after leading India to a T20 World Cup victory, Suryakumar expressed pride in his Mumbai teammate's achievement, highlighting the strong representation from Mumbai cricket.

title: Salim Kumar passes away at 56; Mammootty to Dileep share tributes
content: Renowned Malayalam actor and director Salim Kumar has passed away at 56 after a cardiac arrest. Battling multiple health issues including liver cirrhosis and kidney ailments, the National Award winner was on ventilator support. His demise has deeply saddened the film industry and fans, who remember his versatile performances, from hilarious comedy to poignant drama.

title: Quote of the day by Billie Eilish
content: Billie Eilish, at just 15, emphasized the profound power of lyrics over fleeting musical trends. Her quote, "Words are more powerful than some noises," highlights the lasting impact of meaningful content. This perspective, evident in her own successful career, encourages listeners to focus on substance and truth, ignoring superficial distractions and societal pressures.

title: Shubman Gill scripts history, becomes quickest Indian captain to...
content: Shubman Gill achieved a significant milestone by scoring an unbeaten century against Afghanistan, becoming the 11th Indian captain to surpass 1,000 Test runs. He is the second-fastest Indian captain to reach this mark in innings and the quickest in terms of time, solidifying his impactful leadership.

title: 'Peddi' BO day 3: Ram Charan film sees 7.2 percent growth
content: Ram Charan and Janhvi Kapoor's 'Peddi' is a box office hit, crossing Rs 100 crore net in India within three days. The film's strong performance is bolstered by increasing audience occupancy and growing overseas collections. Meanwhile, alleged chats have surfaced, sparking discussions about Janhvi's character and prompting director Buchi Babu Sana to address audience concerns and promise changes.

title: Indonesian FM Sugiono arrives in New Delhi to co-chair 8th Joint Commission Meeting with EAM Jaishankar
content: Accompanied by Indonesia’s lower house Representative Marlyn Maisarah, Sugionothe Foreign Minister is leading a high-level delegation to the national capital

title: Nagpur NEET aspirant's note not attached to case papers; show-cause notice issued to Investigation Officer
content: “The note left behind by the deseased was mentioned in the station diary entry, but was not attached to the case papers,” said the Deputy Commissioner of Police Nityanand Jha

title: Memories, Medium, or Message? Art as mirror to a world in transition
content: The role of art institutions, how art must respond to a shifting socio-political landscape and its power to shape collective opinion were discussed

title: Tributes paid to Army officer killed during anti-terror operations in J&K's Rajouri
content: The officer died after slipping in a mountainous area and falling into a deep gorge during an anti-terror operation in Rajouri district on Saturday (June 6, 2026) evening

title: Digvijaya Singh urges PM Modi to put three-language policy implementation on hold
content: “I am forwarding herewith a representation received from a group of concerned parents of CBSE Class IX students, opposing the mandatory implementation of the three-language policy in the current mid-session,” Mr. Singh said

title: Why is there a rebellion in the Trinamool Congress? | Explained
content: What event triggered the rebellion within the Trinamool Congress legislature party? Why were MLAs Ritabrata Banerjee and Sandipan Saha expelled from the party? What challenges do Mamata Banerjee and the party face?

title: Domestic LPG price hiked by ₹29 per 14.2-kg cylinder
content: The increase follows a ₹60-per-cylinder hike on March 7 after the conflict in West Asia disrupted global energy supplies and drove up international fuel prices.

title: Some forces trying to push country’s youths towards ‘negative politics’: BJP chief Nitin Nabin
content: “Some forces are trying to make the country's youths anti-establishment,” Mr. Nabin said during an interaction with intellectuals in Ranchi.

title: K. Annamalai | New chapter
content: The former BJP Tamil Nadu president has quit the party and launched a new ‘movement’, promising to bring ‘constructive’ and ‘clean’ politics

title: Versatile actor Salim Kumar passes away aged 57
content: He had been rushed to a private hospital with fever and shortness of breath in the early hours of June 6, and placed on ventilator support

title: Encounter actions in U.P. undermining criminal justice system, making society barbaric, says former U.P. top police officials
content: Sulkhan Singh, a former Uttar Pradesh Director General of Police (DGP) said through encounters the State is normalizing violence, ultimately creating a climate of vigilantism

title: NAC-AP wins dual honours at IINA Awards 2026
content: The awards, presented by Dinesh Sharma, Member of Parliament and former Deputy Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh, recognises contributions to the nation’s infrastructure and skill development sectors

title: TDP announces three candidates for Rajya Sabha elections
content: The TDP stated that the selections were made with a focus on social engineering and dedication and loyalty to the party and to encourage young leadership

title: Pre-monsoon downpour drenches parts of Hyderabad
content: The showers led to waterlogging at multiple locations and slowed vehicular movement; Saturday’s downpour marked the first significant spell of rain ahead of the monsoon season

title: India, Nepal can decisively shift trajectory of bilateral ties to achieve full potential: Jaishankar
content: Nepal wants to pursue “result-driven diplomacy” with India, its “most important partner”, says Nepal FM; “strong complementarities” between the current governments in Delhi, Kathmandu, says EAM

title: CJP warns of nationwide agitation if Education Minister Pradhan not removed in seven days
content: According to CJP spokespersons Ashutosh Ranka, removing the Education Minister was necessary if meaningful reforms were to be undertaken.

title: AIADMK to contest bypolls for 4 Assembly constituencies, says Edappadi K. Palaniswami
content: Speaking at a public meeting to thank the voters of his constituency of Edappadi, he alleges that the TVK is the same as the DMK and the people will soon understand about the TVK government

title: IDFC First Bank-AU Finance Bank fraud case: CBI conducts searches at six locations
content: The searches were carried out on Friday (June 5, 2026) as part of an ongoing probe into the alleged misappropriation of funds parked with IDFC First Bank and AU Finance Bank, an official statement said

title: Delhi Police allowed CJP to protest to ‘pacify’ burgeoning youth anger; Dipke got permission within minutes
content: After analysing the chatter on social media and getting a sense of the situation on the ground, it was felt that refusing permission may have led to some protesters storming the venue or the police station; the confrontation would have further fuelled the anger among the youth, government sources say

title: Why did Supreme Court back curbs on online gaming? | Explained
content: What were the two sets of appeals concerning the regulation and taxation of online gaming platforms in front of the Court? What were the main objections raised by gaming companies against the GST levy? What are the likely consequences for India’s online gaming industry?

title: Shut but open | T.N.’s closure of liquor shops
content: Every time a new party comes to power, closing liquor shops becomes one of its standard promises. But shops are eventually opened in other locations. Is the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam  government’s decision to shut 717 liquor shops near educational institutions and places of worship going to be any different, people wonder

title: News in Frames | Trickle of freshwater just off the salty sea
content: Pits dug on the beach serve as freshwater source for fishing villages near Mimisal in Pudukottai, Tamil Nadu; residents say they are a surviving link to the community’s history, tradition, and resilience; they raise quality concerns over littering

title: Hopes and fears compete as Delhi students join CJP protest for education reforms
content: Student organisations support demand for Education Minister’s resignation but are wary of endorsing the CJP itself, worried about its links to the AAP and the wider goals of the movement

title: A movement losing momentum and daughters as Haryana’s sex ratio declines
content: Launched by PM Modi in Panipat in 2015, the Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao campaign initially delivered strong results. Haryana’s Sex Ratio at Birth rose from 838 in 2010 to 923 by 2019, driven by strict raids on pre-natal testing centres, inter-State crackdowns, monthly video conference reviews, and fixed accountability. However, data for the first four months of 2026 show that the number of girl babies born has dipped below 900, sparking fears that the programme is losing focus and momentum, reports Ashok Kumar

title: From ignominy, Salim Kumar took firm strides to national acclaim
content: In 1997, he was dropped from a film for his ‘lack of acting prowess.’ Thirteen years later, the National Film Award came his way through Adaminte Makan Abu. Salim Kumar, who has acted in 250-odd films, was a lively presence in the Malayalam film industry for three decades, switching effortlessly from comedy to character roles.

title: Odisha police arrest one person from Uttar Pradesh in connection with trafficking of minor girl
content: The girl alleged that she was sold to two families in Jhansi and subjected to sexual assault by multiple men over a period of nearly two years.

title: Two protests, two responses: Delhi stays calm as Haryana turns water cannons on youth Congress demonstrators
content: In Kurukshetra, Congress Lok Sabha MP Deepender Hooda led the demonstration, demanding resignation of the Union Education Minister over the NEET paper leak and alleged irregularities in CBSE examination.

title: Amarinder meets Shah, Nadda amid exit rumours
content: Dismissing reports of a rift, BJP’s senior spokesperson Pritpal Singh Baliawal had said, “He is not going anywhere. He is with the BJP.”

title: Third ro-ro ferry will be launched within a month, says Kochi Mayor
content: The new vessel will have the capacity to accommodate 14 trucks, 12 cars, and 50 passengers and has been designed to address the shortcomings of the existing ro-ro fleet

title: Kerala High Court wins e-governance award
content: DCMS received the Gold Award in the category “Innovation by Use of AI and Other New Age Technologies for Providing Citizen-centric Services (State and State PSUs)” from among 104 entries

title: Metro extension, GIFT City among govt’s top priorities for Ernakulam, says Minister
content: State government will identify the district’s development requirements and expedite key infrastructure projects, says Roji M. John

title: Salim Kumar: the ultimate meme king
content: For three years after winning the National Award in 2011, the genial actor had stayed away from silver screen due to poor health. Yet he remained alive in public memory, thanks largely to memes that liberally drew on his image and witty dialogues, giving them a sharp satirical edge

content: His decision to withdraw the resignation came after a series of meetings with D.K. Shivakumar, Randeep Singh Surjewala and B.K. Hariprasad

content: The art brought Salim Kumar fame and made him a sought-after performer in the famed Kalabhavan troupe and in foreign shows; it paved the way for his entry into the film industry

title: Driver held for illegal transport of river sand near Katpadi
content: The police said that the consignment was proceeding to Chengalpattu district for private contractors from Andhra Pradesh

title: NHAI begins construction of two Foot Over Bridges on Chennai - Bengaluru Highway in Ranipet to prevent accidents
content: NHAI officials said that the new bridges will ensure safe crossing on the stretch by pedestrians without affecting the existing traffic flow on the route

title: District administration gears up for southwest monsoon preparedness in Vellore, nearby districts
content: Revenue officials said that the pre-monsoon summer showers had ensured significant widespread rain in these districts, especially in areas around Jawadhu Hills like Jolarpet and Yelagiri Hills in Tirupattur

content: They also call for establishment of a dedicated Revenue Academy to provide continuous training to enhance professional competence and improve public service delivery

content: Shyamkumar alias Thakaraparambu Kuttan, attacked Rajesh Kumar and his son, who questioned him for blocking the road

content: MPs, MLAs,  and community representatives from across the country would participate in the July 20 rally, Rajendra Pal Gautam, head of the SC department, and Rajya Sabha member Imran Pratapgarhi, who heads the minority department, said at a press conference following a joint convention of the two departments.

title: NCP (SP) MP Sule vows to bring bill to stop horse trading in elections
content: Will move a bill in Parliament seeking structural changes to the electoral process; allegations of horse-trading during elections must stop, says NCP (SP) MP Supriya Sule

content: Various reforms to improve ease of living and ease of doing business were also discussed, official sources said

content: The victim, identified as Anshul Kuncha, was employed with a multinational company and used to work part-time as a pizza delivery executive on weekends to earn extra income.

content: The officer, a Lieutenant, was part of the troops engaged in Operation Sherawali, a search-and-destroy mission that entered its 16th day on Saturday.

content: The woman submitted a written complaint on Friday, alleging that her husband ended their marriage by sending a triple talaq message to her WhatsApp number on May 17.

content: Nepal's Foreign Minister Shishir Khanal kicked off a three-day trip to New Delhi on Friday to strengthen bilateral ties

content: The cost to supply a 14.2-kilogram cylinder has risen to around Rs 1,600-1,700 amid the Middle East conflict.

title: Domestic LPG Prices Raised By Rs 29, Second Hike Since US-Iran War Started
content: The price of a 14.2-kg LPG cylinder in Delhi will rise to Rs 942 from Rs 913 with effect from June 7, according to industry sources.

content: Singapore has long positioned its multicultural model - enshrined in law and national policy - as foundational to its stability and identity.

content: The racket was exposed first in Damoh, where three doctors posted under the National Health Mission at Sanjeevani Clinics were arrested.

content: In a moment of both humanitarian and diplomatic significance, S Jaishankar virtually handed over 72 health facilities and 12 cultural heritage projects reconstructed with Indian development assistance following the devastating 2015 earthquake that killed nearly 9,000 people in Nepal.

title: PM reiterates need to 'add more momentum' to reforms at EAC meet
content: a meeting of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister was held on a wide range of issues relating to India's economic transformation and long-term development priorities . he also shared perspectives on adding more momentum to the reforms journey and ensuring 'ease of living'

title: Sriram Krishnan, Trump's Indian-origin AI brain, to exit White House
content: Sriram Krishnan will leave his post at the end of the month . the Indian-born immigrant helped shape the Trump administration's artificial intelligence strategy . he became one of the most influential voices inside the white house . Krishnan said he will take a break before launching a new initiative .
